item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian retail giant again
item.description: Kyiv’s drones hit warehouses of Russia’s biggest online retailer for the second time in a week on Wednesday, with Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky accusing the e-commerce giant of helping supply the Russian army......Courts& Judiciary

item.title: Dhaka, Beijing discuss proposed transport corridor
item.description: Bangladesh on Wednesday discussed the proposed multi-modal transport corridor with China at a meeting in Manila......

item.title: Flood victims forced to repay microcredit instalments
item.description: Microfinance institutions are allegedly collecting weekly loan instalments from flood victims despite government instructions to suspend such collections in disaster-hit areas, with borrowers saying they had to sell livestock, pawn jewellery or borrow money to meet repayment obligations......

item.title: Trump threatens to destroy Iran bridges, power plants
item.description: US president Donald Trump threatened Wednesday to knock out one Iranian bridge or power plant in return for each attack on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, warning he was ‘not finished’ with a war that has already cost Washington $37.5 billion......

item.title: India protests rage on
item.description: Crowds of supporters of India’s online ‘cockroach’ movement grew on Wednesday at a New Delhi protest site, while their demands for accountability and reform following examination scandals echoed in rallies across the country......GeographicReference

item.title: Govt mulls privatising fuel import, power distribution
item.description: The government is planning to privatise power distribution and allow private firms to import petroleum products to reduce the burden on the national exchequer, said power, energy and mineral resources minister Iqbal Hassan Mahmood Tuku......

item.title: Queues reemerge at fuel pumps
item.description: Long queues formed at filling stations across Rajshahi from Tuesday night amid speculation over a fuel oil supply shortage following the re-escalation of the war in the Middle East......News

item.title: Beximco sukuk faces repayment risk
item.description: Bangladesh’s first private-sector Shariah-based corporate sukuk, worth Tk 3,000 crore, faces the risk of failing to repay investors on time after the Investment Corporation of Bangladesh, acting as trustee, sought regulatory approval to extend its maturity by six years......

item.title: Internships emerge as gateway to jobs for fresh graduates
item.description: Internships are increasingly becoming a key recruitment channel for Bangladesh’s corporate sector, with employers using paid internship programmes to identify talent while helping fresh graduates overcome the experience barrier that keeps many out of the job market......

item.title: Mymensingh seeks investment for agro-processing, livestock
item.description: Local stakeholders at a Bangladesh Investment Development Authority workshop on Wednesday identified livestock, fisheries, agro-eco tourism and agro-processing as key drivers of economic growth in the Mymensingh division......

item.title: ABB seeks waiver from 4pc spread cap on SME loans
item.description: The Association of Bankers, Bangladesh has asked BangladeshBankto exempt small and medium enterprise loans from the newly imposed 4 per cent interest rates spread, arguing that the ceiling would make SME financing commercially unviable......Demographics

item.title: Govt, JICA talk modernising ship recycling industry
item.description: The government and the Japan International Cooperation Agency on Wednesday discussed a range of initiatives to modernise Bangladesh’s ship recycling industry and integrate small and medium-sized enterprises into global supply chains......

item.title: Bangladeshi IT professionals can meet Japan’s tech demand: envoy
item.description: Bangladesh’s young and skilled IT professionals can emerge as reliable partners in meeting Japan’s growing technology sector workforce demand, Bangladesh ambassador to Japan Md Daud Ali said on Wednesday......

item.title: Stocks retreat as late selling wipes out early gains
item.description: The country’s stock market on Wednesday ended lower as strong selling pressure in the final trading hour erased early gains amid persistent policy uncertainties and renewed tensions in the Middle East, dampening investor sentiment......

item.title: BSEC, IMF emphasise expanding green bonds
item.description: The Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission and the International Monetary Fund have discussed expanding green finance and developing thematic bond markets to strengthen climate-resilient investment through the country’s capital market......Environmentclimate change

item.title: Only sectoral reform can end road death
item.description: THE government continues to move in a wrong direction on road safety even as the world makes measurable progress in road traffic death reduction. A recent World Health Organisation report shows that global road fatalities fell by 21 per cent between 2011 and 2025 despite the addition of more than one billion vehicles to the world’s roads, underscoring that sustained......

item.title: Road digging menace in monsoon must end
item.description: INDISCRIMINATE and uncoordinated road digging in Dhaka by city authorities and utility agencies, often prolonged into the monsoon, causes immense suffering to residents every year. As the rains arrive, people in the capital brace themselves for not only water stagnation but also the additional ordeal of roads dug up in a haphazard manner. Such indiscriminate digging also......

item.title: ‘Never again’ means never again
item.description: FOR many Bangladeshis, the phrase ‘never again’ symbolised a national commitment following the July 2024 uprising to break from a painful past marked by enforced disappearances, arbitrary detention, torture and impunity. That commitment has come under renewed scrutiny following Human Rights Watch’s recent report describing the alleged disappearance of......

item.title: Cost of flooding
item.description: A FLOOD in Bangladesh is never only water. It is a farmer’s crop submerged days before harvest, a shopkeeper’s stock ruined overnight, a road cut off from the nearest market and a family forced to borrow simply to eat. Seasonal inundation has always sustained the delta, renewing soils, fisheries and wetlands. But when floods become deeper, faster or less......

item.title: Recognition must go beyond symbolism
item.description: FOR decades, Bangladesh has celebrated its migrant workers with lofty phrases. They are called remittance warriors, economic lifelines and silent nation builders. Every year, when remittance figures climb despite global uncertainty, speeches become more generous and gratitude more visible. Yet appreciation has rarely translated into institutional dignity. The......

item.title: More than growth
item.description: BANGLADESH has reached an important moment in its economic journey. As the country prepares to graduate from Least Developed Country status, the narrative of being a development success story is no longer enough. Sustained economic growth may have improved incomes and infrastructure, but it has not translated into a stronger global identity......

item.title: Biman claims pilot appointment complied with regulations
item.description: Biman Bangladesh Airlines has claimed that the appointment of its pilots complied with the regulations of the Civil Aviation Authority of Bangladesh and that passenger safety was not compromised......News

item.title: From threats to vandalism: July women’s monument under attack
item.description: The country’s first monument dedicated to women who took part in the July uprising has remained a target, with vandalism reported months after its inauguration was delayed following threats from what authorities described as ‘right-wing individuals’......

item.title: Safety audit finds ‘serious deficiencies’ in metro rail
item.description: Serious structural and operational shortcomings have surfaced in the country’s metro rail system after its first-ever independent safety audit, leading the HighCourtto order the implementation of the audit committee’s recommendations within weeks......

item.title: CPB opposes divestment of 44 state mills
item.description: Leaders of the Communist Party of Bangladesh on Wednesday urged the government to halt its plan to divest 44 state-owned mills and factories and scrap the trade deal with the United States signed during the interim administration......

item.title: NCP raises trade deal concerns with US envoy
item.description: The National Citizen Party on Wednesday raised its concerns over the recent United States-Bangladesh trade agreement during a meeting with US ambassador Brent T Christensen......Demographics

item.title: ‘Cholo Shoi’ expands horizon of kinship, community
item.description: Curated by Parsa Sanjana Sajid, the group collage art exhibition ‘Cholo Shoi’ is about kinship beyond the identity of sex and gender......

item.title: ECNEC approves Dhaka-Ashulia expressway project cost hike by 55pc
item.description: State minister for planning Zonayed Abdur Rahim Saki said on Wednesday that the ongoing initiatives taken to accelerate the preparation and implementation of development projects would be completed in 180 days of the incumbent government that assumed office in February......

item.title: Cabinet body clears Tk 7.34cr contract variation for RUET project
item.description: The Cabinet Committee on Government Purchase on Wednesday approved a proposal to increase the cost of constructing a 10-storey administrative building under the ‘Further Development of Rajshahi University of Engineering and Technology (2nd Revised)’ project by Tk 7.34 crore......
